title: 30-Day Frontend Prep
description: From JavaScript fundamentals to system design — a structured path to crack frontend interviews at top companies.

steps.title: JavaScript Core: Closures & Scope
steps.description: Master closures, lexical scope, the event loop, and how JavaScript executes code. These come up in almost every JS interview.

steps.title: JavaScript: Prototypes & Inheritance
steps.description: Understand prototype chains, Object.create, and why JS inheritance differs from classical OOP.

steps.title: JavaScript: Async Patterns
steps.description: Callbacks, Promises, async/await — and common pitfalls like Promise.all vs Promise.allSettled.

steps.title: JavaScript: ES6+ Features
steps.description: Arrow functions, destructuring, spread/rest, generators, WeakMap/WeakSet — the modern JS toolbox.

steps.title: CSS: Flexbox & Grid Deep Dive
steps.description: Practice 5 CSS layout challenges. Know display:flex vs display:grid, alignment, and responsive design patterns.

steps.title: React: Virtual DOM & Reconciliation
steps.description: How React's diffing algorithm works, why keys matter in lists, and what causes unnecessary re-renders.

steps.title: React: Hooks Deep Dive
steps.description: useEffect cleanup, useMemo vs useCallback, custom hooks, and the rules of hooks.

steps.title: DSA: Arrays & Two Pointers
steps.description: Solve 5 problems using the two-pointer technique: two-sum, container with most water, trapping rain water.

steps.title: DSA: Sliding Window Pattern
steps.description: Maximum subarray sum, longest substring without repeating characters, minimum window substring.

steps.title: DSA: Trees & Recursion
steps.description: Binary tree traversals (BFS, DFS), lowest common ancestor, validate BST, serialize/deserialize.

steps.title: Browser: Rendering Pipeline
steps.description: How the browser parses HTML/CSS, builds the DOM and CSSOM, and paints pixels. Critical rendering path optimization.

steps.title: Browser: Performance & Core Web Vitals
steps.description: LCP, FID, CLS. Code splitting, lazy loading, debounce/throttle, and reducing main thread blocking.

steps.title: Security: XSS, CSRF & CSP
steps.description: How XSS and CSRF attacks work, how to prevent them, and what Content Security Policy does.

steps.title: System Design: Design a URL Shortener
steps.description: Your first frontend-relevant system design. Cover hashing, redirects, analytics, and scaling read-heavy workloads.

steps.title: System Design: Design an Autocomplete
steps.description: Trie data structure, debouncing search input, caching suggestions, and progressive enhancement.

steps.title: OOPs: SOLID Principles with Examples
steps.description: Single Responsibility, Open/Closed, Liskov Substitution — with concrete frontend examples using React components.

steps.title: Behavioral: Tell Me About Yourself + Past Projects
steps.description: Craft your 2-minute intro. Prepare 3 project stories using STAR format (Situation, Task, Action, Result).

steps.title: Mock Round: 5 Frontend Interview Questions
steps.description: Answer 5 mixed frontend questions (2 JS, 1 React, 1 browser, 1 behavioral) as if in a real interview. Time yourself.

steps.title: Company Research: Read Top 3 Company Question Banks
steps.description: Go through question banks for the top companies you're targeting. Note patterns in what they ask.

steps.title: Final Review: Revision List + Weak Areas
steps.description: Go through your Revision List, re-read any concepts you got wrong, and do a final mock round.
